goal: Build an inspect-and-reject station: 5 cubes on conveyor, 4 marked 'good' (semantic class) and 1 unmarked (defective). 'good' cubes route to GoodBin via color_routing; the unmarked cube falls through to DEST_PATH = RejectBin. Demonstrates the 2-path quality-gate pattern using fall-through behavior in cuRobo handler's color_routing.
thoughts:
Six inspect-and-reject specifics for CP-18 (extends CP-17 — uses semantic class but adds fall-through path):
1) 5 cubes total: 4 tagged with set_semantic_label(class_name='good', semantic_type='class'), 1 untagged (no Semantics API applied). Untagged cube has _cube_semantic_class returning None.
2) color_routing dict has ONE entry: {'good': '/World/GoodBin'}. Untagged cube finds no match → handler falls through to destination_path (DEST_PATH = '/World/RejectBin').
3) Visual: 'good' cubes green (passed inspection), bad cube red (rejected). Physical color is independent of semantic class — visual aid only.
4) RejectBin acts as the fall-through destination. setup_pick_place_controller's destination_path arg = '/World/RejectBin'. color_routing only fires for 'good' cubes; everything else goes to RejectBin.
5) Bin layout: GoodBin behind robot at +X side (y=-0.4, x=0.3), RejectBin at -X side (y=-0.4, x=-0.3). Both within reach.
6) The 1 unmarked cube simulates a defect detected by inspection station. Real-world use: vision_classify_cubes upstream tags 'good' cubes; defective cubes left unlabeled trigger reject path.
code:
# DomeLight + Ground + Cell + Table
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.0, 0.5, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    position=[0.0, 0.4, 0.78],
    size=[3.0, 0.4, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.2, 0, 0],
)

# 5 cubes — 4 good (green + 'good' class), 1 defective (red, no class)
good_specs = [
    ("/World/Cube_g1", -1.4),
    ("/World/Cube_g2", -1.1),
    ("/World/Cube_g3", -0.5),
    ("/World/Cube_g4", -0.2),
]
bad_spec = ("/World/Cube_bad", -0.8)

create_material(material_path="/World/Materials/Green", shader_type="OmniPBR", diffuse_color=[0.0, 1.0, 0.0])
create_material(material_path="/World/Materials/Red", shader_type="OmniPBR", diffuse_color=[1.0, 0.0, 0.0])

for path, x in good_specs:
    create_prim(prim_path=path, prim_type="Cube", position=[x, 0.4, 0.835], size=0.05)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)
    assign_material(prim_path=path, material_path="/World/Materials/Green")
    set_semantic_label(prim_path=path, class_name="good", semantic_type="class")

# Bad cube — red, NO semantic label (will fall through color_routing)
bad_path, bad_x = bad_spec
create_prim(prim_path=bad_path, prim_type="Cube", position=[bad_x, 0.4, 0.835], size=0.05)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path=bad_path, schema_name=api)
assign_material(prim_path=bad_path, material_path="/World/Materials/Red")
# (intentionally NO set_semantic_label here)

all_cubes = [s[0] for s in good_specs] + [bad_path]
bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=all_cubes,
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
for c in all_cubes:
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=c, material_name="rubber")

# Two bins
create_bin(prim_path="/World/GoodBin",   position=[ 0.3, -0.4, 0.75], size=[0.25, 0.25, 0.15])
create_bin(prim_path="/World/RejectBin", position=[-0.3, -0.4, 0.75], size=[0.25, 0.25, 0.15])
assign_material(prim_path="/World/GoodBin",   material_path="/World/Materials/Green")
assign_material(prim_path="/World/RejectBin", material_path="/World/Materials/Red")

add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/PickSensor", position=[0.4, 0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])

# color_routing has ONLY 'good' — bad cube falls through to destination_path (RejectBin)
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    belt_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    source_paths=all_cubes,
    destination_path="/World/RejectBin",
    color_routing={
        "good": "/World/GoodBin",
    },
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/ConveyorBelt", "/World/GoodBin", "/World/RejectBin"],
)
extension_notes: CP-18 demonstrates the fall-through behavior of color_routing: when a cube's semantic class isn't in the routing dict (or has no semantic class at all), the controller routes to destination_path. Useful for binary inspection (good/reject) or any 'default + exceptions' pattern. Future: use add_vision_classifier_gate (when render-flush is fixed) to dynamically tag cubes upstream of the pick station instead of pre-tagging.
failure_modes: Bad cube has Semantics_color or Semantics_colour applied accidentally — set_semantic_label MUST not be called for the bad cube. Checked at canonical-author time.
color_routing match key mismatch (e.g. 'Good' instead of 'good') — handler lowercases cube's class_name before lookup but exact match preferred.
Bad cube path not in source_paths — controller wouldn't see it, would never pick. Must include in source_paths.
Both bins identical color — visual ambiguity. Use distinct color materials (green + red here).
verified_status: build-spec-2026-04-XX; form-gate ✓; function-gate ✓ (150s sim. cube_final=(0.23, -0.49, 0.785) in Bin)
